submission_methods.reasoning: All sources consistently show portal as fastest (3-5 days), fax as standard (5-7 days), and phone for urgent only. Provider manual states 'For urgent authorization requests only' for phone, web page shows 'PORTAL (FASTEST)' and 'PHONE (URGENT)', indicating clear preference order.

fax_number.reasoning: All four sources agree on (844) 405-0296. Phone rep on March 20, 2026 explicitly confirmed 'For medical drug PAs, the fax is (844) 405-0296. That hasn't changed.' Denial letter from March 18, 2026 also references this number for resubmission.

portal_url.reasoning: Provider manual shows full URL 'www.anthem.com/providers' while web page and denial letter show shortened 'anthem.com/providers'. The provider manual's full URL format is more complete and appropriate for official documentation. All point to the same destination.

phone_status_only.reasoning: Phone transcript shows IVR path: 'For providers, press 2' then 'For prior authorization, press 1' then 'For medical drug and infusion authorizations, press 1'. This is the path to check authorization status and submit new requests.

phone_urgent.reasoning: All sources consistently reference (800) 274-7767 for urgent requests. Provider manual specifies 'For urgent authorization requests only' and web page shows 'PHONE (URGENT): (800) 274-7767'.

phone_hours.reasoning: Only provider manual provides phone hours: 'M-F 8am-6pm local time'. No other sources contradict this information.

required_documents.reasoning: Provider manual provides comprehensive list: 'Completed PA form (ANT-MED-PA-25), Office visit notes (within 60 days), Relevant labs/diagnostics, Letter of medical necessity, Current medication list'. Web page checklist confirms these same requirements. Phone transcript emphasizes completeness to avoid pending status.

chart_note_window_days.reasoning: Policy updated from 60 to 90 days in November 2025. Web page explicitly states 'Office notes from most recent visit (within 90 days — updated Nov 2025)'. Denial letter confirms '90-day documentation window per our November 2025 policy update'. Provider manual (60 days) and phone transcript (60 days) are outdated, predating the November 2025 policy change.

superseded_values.reason: Policy updated to 90 days in November 2025 per web page and denial letter confirmation

lab_window_days.reasoning: No sources specify a time window for lab results. All sources mention 'relevant labs/diagnostics' as required but do not specify age restrictions.

turnaround_standard_days.reasoning: All three sources that specify standard turnaround agree on 5 business days. Provider manual: 'Standard: 5 business days', phone transcript: 'Standard 5 business days from complete submission', web page: 'Standard: 5 business days'.

turnaround_portal_days.reasoning: Both provider manual and web page specify '3-5 business days' for portal submissions. Provider manual states 'Turnaround: 3-5 business days' under portal section, web page shows 'Average turnaround: 3-5 business days' under portal.

turnaround_fax_days.reasoning: Both provider manual and web page specify '5-7 business days' for fax submissions. Provider manual states 'Turnaround: 5-7 business days' under fax section, web page shows 'Average turnaround: 5-7 business days' under fax.

turnaround_urgent_hours.reasoning: Both provider manual and web page specify 24 hours for urgent requests. Provider manual: 'Urgent: 24 hours', web page: 'Urgent/Expedited: 24 hours'.

pend_period_days.reasoning: No sources specify an auto-denial deadline for incomplete submissions. Phone transcript mentions pending but doesn't specify timeframe.

appeal_fax.reasoning: Both provider manual and denial letter specify (844) 405-0299 for appeals. Provider manual: 'Fax: (844) 405-0299', denial letter: 'Fax appeals to: (844) 405-0299'.

appeal_phone.reasoning: Provider manual and denial letter both specify '(800) 274-7767, Option 5' for appeals. Web page shows different path '(800) 274-7767 → Option 1 → Option 1' but this appears to be for general PA inquiries, not appeals specifically.

superseded_values.reason: Web page path is for general PA inquiries, not appeals

appeal_deadline_days.reasoning: Denial letter specifies 'Deadline: 180 days from this notice' for appeals. This is the most authoritative source for appeal deadline information.

appeal_mail.reasoning: No sources provide a mailing address for written appeals.

system_migration_in_progress.reasoning: No sources mention any system migration or transition period. All systems appear to be operating normally.

common_denial_reasons.reasoning: Web page provides specific denial statistics. Phone transcript corroborates this, noting 'we're seeing a lot of submissions come in missing the HER2 results or without the biosimilar justification, and then it gets pended'.

coverage_states.reasoning: Provider manual explicitly states 'This guide covers Anthem BCBS commercial plans in CA, NY, OH, GA, IN, and VA. For other Blue plans, contact the local plan directly.'

pa_form.reasoning: All four sources consistently specify form ANT-MED-PA-25. No version conflicts or supersession detected.

step_therapy_required.reasoning: Provider manual explicitly states 'Step therapy required (biosimilar first for new starts)' and web page requires 'Biosimilar step therapy attestation'. Both sources confirm step therapy requirement.

prior_treatment_failure_required.reasoning: Provider manual specifies 'Prior DMARD trial required for RA indication'. This is indication-specific requirement for rheumatoid arthritis.

biosimilar_requirement.reasoning: Both provider manual and web page specify biosimilar requirement. Provider manual: 'biosimilar first for new starts', web page: 'Biosimilar step therapy attestation'.
